course_description:
MATH7333 covers key topics in group theory and ring theory, with a view towards commutative algebra, algebraic number theory and representation theory.

This course covers topics fromﾠ modern algebraﾠ, especially groups and rings.ﾠ This is an essential course for any student wishing to undertake further study in pure mathematics and mathematical physics.

The timetable for this course is available on the

Additional timetable information
There will be no lectures or practicals on public holidays.
course_aims: On successfulﾠcompletion of this course studentsﾠshould have a solid understanding of the fundamentals of groups, rings and fields.

learning_outcomes.description: Group theory - After completion of this course students should have a solid understanding of the essentials of modern group theory. Topics covered include the three isomorphism theorems, the notion of solvable and simple groups, the JordanHlder theorem and the extension problem.

Ring theory - After completion of this course students should have a solid understanding of basic ring theory. Topics covered include ideals, the characteristic of a ring, the isomorphism theorems for rings, domains and integral domains, fraction fields and unique factorsation domains.

assessment_details.title: Assignments
assessment_details.task_description: There will be 4 assignments, each worth 7.5%. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit using Gradescope.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Solutions will be released after 7 days so that is the maximum extension possible.
See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for extension/deferral information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Quiz 1
assessment_details.task_description: Quiz 1 will be conducted in class. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit in the lecture.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
If you miss either quiz 1 or 2 for a valid reason (e.g. with a medical certificate), then your other two quizzes will count for more. If you miss two quizzes or more, or if you miss quiz 3, then you must do an oral examination.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Lecture classes Three traditional black/whiteboard lectures per week. Students are expected to actively participate by taking notes, asking questions, and by answering weekly homework problems.
Practicals During the practical students can work on the assignment problems (some of which are deliberately hard and require assistance from the tutor).
